construct.label: Somatic and ambient AI anxiety anchor
construct.definition: Anxiety arising from diffuse bodily unease, vigilance, sleep disruption, or background physiological arousal after exposure to AI capability news or AI-mediated environments.
construct.subconstruct: ambient bodily unease after AI news exposure

scenario_text: Imagine that during the same week you read several credible news stories about AI systems passing difficult exams, producing realistic video, automating parts of professional work, and being tested in hospitals, schools, and public agencies. None of the stories directly affects your job or family. Still, later that evening you notice a tight chest, restless attention, and difficulty settling down because the news makes the future feel less predictable.

response_scale.prompt: How anxious would this scenario make you in terms of bodily unease or background tension after AI news exposure?

scoring.missing_response_rule: Treat skipped responses as missing at the item level. Do not impute single-item scores for primary analyses.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude respondents who fail the survey-level attention check specified in the study protocol.
Exclude respondents who complete the item in less than one-third of the median reading time for the administration wave.
Exclude respondents who report that they could not understand the scenario after reading it.
Exclude respondents younger than the minimum eligible age for the study population.

interpretation_bands.label: low
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports little or no anxiety-related bodily unease after exposure to broad AI capability news.

interpretation_bands.label: moderate
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports a meaningful but not severe anxiety response involving diffuse bodily tension or unsettled attention.

interpretation_bands.label: high
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports strong ambient or somatic anxiety in response to cumulative AI capability news and future uncertainty.

population.target_population: Adults in the general population, with planned subgroup analysis by baseline anxiety history, news exposure, health-anxiety sensitivity, and frequency of AI-related media consumption.
population.eligible_respondents: Respondents aged 18 or older who can complete the survey language without assistance and provide informed consent.
exclusion_criteria: Current inability to provide informed consent.
Duplicate panel participation within the same measurement wave.
Self-reported inability to read the survey language.

administration_mode.instructions: Present the scenario text exactly as written, followed immediately by the response prompt and the five ordered response options.
administration_mode.randomization: Randomize item order within the cross-domain anchor pool while preserving the ordered response scale.
administration_mode.longitudinal_use: Use identical wording, response anchors, scoring, and eligibility rules across waves unless a new item version is released.

ethics_flags.data_protection_notes: Do not collect medical diagnoses, medication details, clinical records, or identifiable mental-health treatment history for this item.
ethics_flags.irb_notes: The item references mild bodily anxiety symptoms and should include a reminder that respondents may skip items. It is not a clinical diagnostic item and should not be interpreted as measuring an anxiety disorder.
